channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Pistons are back in the playoffs and take NBA-record postseason losing streak into Game 1 vs Knicks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York beating Detroit had become something of an NBA certainty, with the Knicks doing it 16 straight times over a span of five years. The Pistons finally ended that streak with three wins over the Knicks during their massive 2024-25 turnaround season.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NHL sets another attendance record, topping 23 million fans for the 1st time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NHL has set another attendance record and surpassed 23 million fans for the first time. The league on Friday reported an attendance figure of 23,014,458 over the 1,312-game regular season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Maine Democrats trying to amend state constitution to codify allowing trans athletes in girls' sports
item.description: Maine Democrats took a step toward constitutionalizing trans athletes' protections the day after the DOJ announced a lawsuit against the state over the issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says he's not holding teams hostage as he ponders his future in the NFL
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is in no rush to make a decision about his future. The free-agent quarterback said during an appearance on “The Pat McAfee Show” on Thursday that he is “not holding anybody hostage" as he weighs whether to return for a 21st season. Rodgers was released by the New York Jets in March.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White Sox lose 8-0 after calling up Top 100 prospect, reinstating Benintendi from IL
item.description: The Athletics defeated the White Sox 8-0 behind four home runs from four different players and two run-scoring doubles from shortstop Jacob Wilson, despite the White Sox calling up prized catching prospect Edgar Quero and reinstating outfielder Andrew Benintendi from the 10-day injured list.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: WWE superstar Roman Reigns shows 'support' for Trump ahead of WrestleMania
item.description: Roman Reigns, despite being a registered Democrat in Florida, said the choice for this past election was 'very clear,' and he 'support[s' Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rams' draft headquarters to be at LAFD Air Operations base to honor first responders to wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams will make their selections in the upcoming NFL draft from the Los Angeles Fire Department's Air Operations base in Van Nuys to honor the first responders who stopped the deadly wildfires that ravaged Southern California earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nats pitcher Jorge López suspended 3 games for throwing at Pittsburgh's Andrew McCutchen's head
item.description: Washington Nationals pitcher Jorge López has been suspended three games and fined for throwing at Pittsburgh's Andrew McCutchen, Major League Baseball announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NCAA panel gives final OK to rule designed to discourage football players from faking injuries
item.description: The NCAA Playing Rules Oversight Panel gave final approval to a rule designed to discourage football players from faking injuries to stop the game clock, the NCAA announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warriors-Rockets schedule for first-round playoff series
item.description: (KRON) -- They took the hard way in, but the Golden State Warriors are officially in the playoffs after securing a spot in Tuesday's nail-biting win over the Memphis Grizzlies. The No. 7 seed Dubs (48-34) will face the No. 2 seed Houston Rockets (52-30) in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Warriors host Memphis Grizzlies in NBA play-in game Tuesday at Chase Center
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (BCN) -- The Golden State Warriors missed a chance to clinch a National Basketball Association playoff spot in their last regular season game on Sunday but will get another chance when they host a play-in game Tuesday against the Memphis Grizzlies at Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Cubs can't hold lead, MLB-best Padres surge late to win opening game of 3-game series in San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Cubs' West Coast gauntlet against two of the best teams in baseball rolls on this week, with two games left.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Sky select Ajsa Sivka, Hailey Van Lith in WNBA Draft
item.description: The Chicago Sky selected Slovenian stretch four Ajsa Sivka and TCU point guard Hailey Van Lith with their top two picks in the WNBA draft to address their need for perimeter shooting and to bolster their rebounding efforts.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Crow-Armstrong homers twice and Cubs take series with 4-2 victory over Dodgers
item.description: Pete Crow-Armstrong hit his first two home runs of the season, including a go-ahead solo shot in the seventh inning, Michael Busch homered against his former team again and the Chicago Cubs beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 4-2 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah scores four power play goals in 5-3 victory
item.description: DALLAS (AP) - Logan Cooley had a goal and two assists to lead the Utah Hockey Club to a 5-3 win against the Dallas Stars at American Airlines Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Thunder crush Jazz in home finale, 145-111
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - The Utah Jazz home finale was pretty representative of the entire season. The Jazz got blown out by the Oklahoma City Thunder.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Phil Wertman Invitational: GJ's Bryce Flanagan dominates 800m and more
item.description: The sun scorched down on Stocker Stadium Friday for the Phil Wertman Invitational. But the heat did not stop many of the Western Slope's top athletes from showing off their speed, power and verticals.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
